khan_exercises.title: Linear equations with unknown coefficients
khan_exercises.description: Solve a linear equation where the coefficients are represented by letters.
topic_path: Get ready for differentiation: composite, implicit, and inverse functions

items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$.**  
*Assume the equation has a solution for $x$.*

$ax+3x = bx+5$

$x=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $x$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $x$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $x$.

###Solving for $x$

$\begin{align} ax+3x &= bx+5  \\\\
ax+3x-bx &= 5 & (\text{Subtract } bx \text{ from both sides}) \\\\ 
x(a+3-b) &= 5 & (\text{Factor out }x) \\\\ 
x &= \dfrac{5}{a+3-b} & (\text{Divide both sides by } a+3-b)\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###The answer

$x = \dfrac{5}{a+3-b} $

items.question_content:
**Solve for $y$.**  
*Assume the equation has a solution for $y$.*

$py+qy= -4y+8$

$y=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $y$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $y$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $y$.

###Solving for $y$

$\begin{align} py+qy&= -4y+8 \\\\
py+qy+4y&= 8 & (\text{Add } 4y \text{ to both sides}) \\\\ 
y(p+q+4) &= 8 & (\text{Factor out }y) \\\\ 
y &= \dfrac{8}{p+q+4} & (\text{Divide both sides by } p+q+4)\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###The answer

$y = \dfrac{8}{p+q+4} $

items.question_content:
**Solve for $z$.**  
*Assume the equation has a solution for $z$.*

$-cz+6z= tz+83$

$z=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $z$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $z$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $z$.

###Solving for $z$

$\begin{align} -cz+6z&= tz+83  \\\\
-cz+6z-tz&= 83 & (\text{Subtract } tz \text{ from both sides}) \\\\ 
z(-c+6-t) &= 83 & (\text{Factor out }z) \\\\ 
z &= \dfrac{83}{-c+6-t} & (\text{Divide both sides by } -c+6-t)\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###The answer

$z = \dfrac{83}{-c+6-t} $

items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$.**  
*Assume the equation has a solution for $x$.*

$19x+rx= -37x+w$

$x=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $x$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $x$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $x$.

###Solving for $x$

$\begin{align} 19x+rx&= -37x+w  \\\\
37x+19x+rx&= w & (\text{Add } 37x \text{  to both sides}) \\\\ 
56x+rx&=w\\\\
x(56+r) &= w & (\text{Factor out }x) \\\\ 
x &= \dfrac{w}{56+r} & (\text{Divide both sides by } 56+r)\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###The answer

$x = \dfrac{w}{56+r} $

items.question_content:
**Solve for $z$.**  
*Assume the equation has a solution for $z$.*

$16z+29z= pz-v$

$z=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $z$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $z$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $z$.

###Solving for $z$

$\begin{align} 16z+29z&= pz-v  \\\\
45z-pz&= -v & (\text{Subtract } pz \text{ from both sides}) \\\\ 
z(45-p) &= -v & (\text{Factor out }z) \\\\ 
z &= \dfrac{-v}{45-p} & (\text{Divide both sides by } 45-p)\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###The answer

$z = \dfrac{-v}{45-p} $

items.question_content:
**Solve for $y$.**  
*Assume the equation has a solution for $y$.*

$py+7 = 6y+q$

$y=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $y$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $y$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $y$.

###Solving for $y$

$\begin{align} py+7 &= 6y+q  \\\\
py+7 -6y &= q & (\text{Subtract } 6y \text{ from both sides}) \\\\ 
py-6y&= q - 7& (\text{Subtract } 7 \text{ from both sides})\\\\
y(p-6) &= q-7 & (\text{Factor out }y) \\\\ 
y &= \dfrac{q-7}{p-6} & (\text{Divide both sides by } p-6)\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###The answer

$y = \dfrac{q-7}{p-6}$

items.question_content:
**Solve for $z$.**  
*Assume the equation has a solution for $z$.*

$-4z+1 = bz+c$

$z=$
 [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $z$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $z$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $z$.

###Solving for $z$

$\begin{align} -4z+1 &= bz+c  \\\\
-4z+1 -bz &= c & (\text{Subtract } bz \text{ from both sides}) \\\\ 
-4z-bz&= c -1& (\text{Subtract } 1 \text{ from both sides})\\\\
z(-4-b) &= c-1 & (\text{Factor out }z) \\\\ 
z &= \dfrac{c-1}{-4-b} & (\text{Divide both sides by } -4-b)\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###The answer

$z = \dfrac{c-1}{-4-b}$

items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$.**  
*Assume the equation has a solution for $x$.*

$-px+r = -8x-2$

$x=$
 [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $x$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $x$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $x$.

###Solving for $x$

$\begin{align} -px+r &= -8x-2  \\\\
-px+r +8x &= -2 & (\text{Add } 8x \text{ to both sides}) \\\\ 
-px+8x&= -2 - r& (\text{Subtract } r \text{ from both sides})\\\\
x(-p+8) &= -2-r & (\text{Factor out }x) \\\\ 
x &= \dfrac{-2-r}{-p+8} & (\text{Divide both sides by } -p+8)\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###The answer

$x = \dfrac{-2-r}{-p+8}$

items.question_content:
**Solve for $y$.**  
*Assume the equation has a solution for $y$.*

$12y+d = -19y+t$

$y=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $y$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $y$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $y$.

###Solving for $y$

$\begin{align} 12y+d &= -19y+t  \\\\
19y+12y+d  &= t & (\text{Add } 19y \text{ to both sides}) \\\\ 
31y&= t - d& (\text{Subtract } d \text{ from both sides})\\\\
y &= \dfrac{t-d}{31} & (\text{Divide both sides by } 31)\end{align}$

###The answer

$y = \dfrac{t-d}{31}$

items.question_content:
**Solve for $z$.**  
*Assume the equation has a solution for $z$.*

$az+17 = -4z-b$

$z=$
 [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $z$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $z$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $z$.

###Solving for $z$

$\begin{align} az+17 &= -4z-b  \\\\
az+17 +4z &= -b & (\text{Add } 4z \text{ to both sides}) \\\\ 
az+4z&= -b - 17& (\text{Subtract } 17 \text{ from both sides})\\\\
z(a+4)&= -b-17 & (\text{Factor out }z) \\\\ 
z &= \dfrac{-b-17}{a+4} & (\text{Divide both sides by } a+4)\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###The answer

$z = \dfrac{-b-17}{a+4}$

items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$.**  
*Assume the equation has a solution for $x$.*

$a\cdot(5-x) = bx-8$

$x=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $x$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $x$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $x$.

###Solving for $x$

$\begin{align} a(5-x) &= bx-8  \\\\
5a-ax&=bx-8&(\text{Distribute over } a)\\\\
5a-ax-bx &= -8 & (\text{Subtract } bx \text{ from both sides}) \\\\ 
-ax-bx&=-8-5a&(\text{Subtract } 5a \text{ from both sides})\\\\
x(-a-b) &= -8-5a& (\text{Factor out }x) \\\\ 
x &= \dfrac{-8-5a}{-a-b} & (\text{Divide both sides by } -a-b)\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###The answer

$x = \dfrac{-8-5a}{-a-b} $

items.question_content:
**Solve for $y$.**  
*Assume the equation has a solution for $y$.*

$a\cdot(n+y) = 10y+32$

$y=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $y$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $y$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $y$.

###Solving for $y$

$\begin{align} a(n+y) &= 10y+32  \\\\
an+ay&=10y+32&(\text{Distribute over } a)\\\\
an+ay-10y &= 32 & (\text{Subtract } 10y \text{ from both sides}) \\\\ 
ay-10y&=32-an&(\text{Subtract } an \text{ from both sides})\\\\
y(a-10) &= 32-an & (\text{Factor out }y) \\\\ 
y &= \dfrac{32-an}{a-10} & (\text{Divide both sides by } a-10)\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###The answer

$y = \dfrac{32-an}{a-10} $

items.question_content:
**Solve for $z$.**  
*Assume the equation has a solution for $z$.*

$w\cdot(-4+z) = mz+17$

$z=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $z$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $z$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $z$.

###Solving for $z$

$\begin{align} w(-4+z) &= mz+17  \\\\
-4w+wz&=mz+17&(\text{Distribute over } w)\\\\
-4w+wz-mz &= 17 & (\text{Subtract } mz \text{ from both sides}) \\\\ 
wz-mz&=17+4w&(\text{Add } 4w \text{ to both sides})\\\\
z(w-m) &= 17+4w & (\text{Factor out }z) \\\\ 
z &= \dfrac{17+4w}{w-m} & (\text{Divide both sides by } w-m)\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###The answer

$z = \dfrac{17+4w}{w-m} $

items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$.**  
*Assume the equation has a solution for $x$.*

$n\cdot(17+x) = 34x-r$

$x=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $x$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $x$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $x$.

###Solving for $x$

$\begin{align} n(17+x) &= 34x-r  \\\\
17n+nx&=34x-r&(\text{Distribute over } n)\\\\
17n+nx-34x &= -r & (\text{Subtract } 34x \text{ from both sides}) \\\\ 
nx-34x&=-r-17n&(\text{Subtract } 17n \text{ from both sides})\\\\
x(n-34) &= -r-17n & (\text{Factor out }x) \\\\ 
x &= \dfrac{-r-17n}{n-34} & (\text{Divide both sides by } n-34)\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###The answer

z(a-45) &= 67-ta& (\text{Factor out }z) \\\\ 
z &= \dfrac{67-ta}{a-45} & (\text{Divide both sides by } a-45)\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###The answer

$z = \dfrac{67-ta}{a-45} $

items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$.**  
*Assume the equation has a solution for $x$.*

$ax+3x = bx+5$

$x=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $x$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $x$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $x$.

###Solving for $x$

$\begin{align} ax+3x &= bx+5  \\\\
ax+3x-bx &= 5 & (\text{Subtract } bx \text{ from both sides}) \\\\ 
x(a+3-b) &= 5 & (\text{Factor out }x) \\\\ 
x &= \dfrac{5}{a+3-b} & (\text{Divide both sides by } a+3-b)\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###The answer

$x = \dfrac{5}{a+3-b} $

items.question_content:
**Solve for $s$.**

$0.5s+1=7+4.5s$

$s =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: We need to manipulate the equation to get $   s   $ by itself.

$\begin{align} 0.5s+1&=7+4.5s \\\\
0.5s+1 \blueD{-4.5s} &= 7+4.5s\blueD{-4.5s} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract 4.5s from each side.}}\\\\
-4s+1&=7 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
-4s+1\blueD{-1} &= 7\blueD{-1} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract 1 from each side.}}\\\\
-4s &=6 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{-4s}{\blueD{-4}} &= \dfrac{6}{\blueD{-4}} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by -4}.}\\\\
s &= \greenD{-1.5} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$s = \greenD{    -1.5 }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]

items.question_content:

$4a+5=2+3.25a$

$a =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: We need to manipulate the equation to get $   a   $ by itself.

$\begin{align} 4a+5&=2+3.25a \\\\
4a+5 \blueD{-3.25a} &= 2+3.25a\blueD{-3.25a} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract 3.25a from each side.}}\\\\
0.75a+5&=2 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
0.75a+5\blueD{-5} &= 2\blueD{-5} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract 5 from each side.}}\\\\
0.75a &=-3 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{0.75a}{\blueD{0.75}} &= \dfrac{-3}{\blueD{0.75}} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by 0.75.}}\\\\
a &= \greenD{-4} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$a = \greenD{ -4    }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align} 4.5+1.5k&=18-3k \\\\
4.5+1.5k \blueD{+3k} &= 18-3k\blueD{+3k} &&\small\gray{\text{Add 3k to each side.}}\\\\
4.5k+4.5&=18 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
4.5k+4.5\blueD{-4.5} &= 18\blueD{-4.5} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract 4.5 from each side.}}\\\\
4.5k &=13.5 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{4.5k}{\blueD{4.5}} &= \dfrac{13.5}{\blueD{4.5}} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by 4.5.}}\\\\
k &= \greenD{3} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$k = \greenD{  3   }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]

items.question_content:
**Solve for $m$.**

$12.6+4m=9.6+8m$

$m =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: We need to manipulate the equation to get $    m  $ by itself.

$\begin{align} 12.6+4m&=9.6+8m \\\\
12.6+4m \blueD{-8m} &= 9.6+8m\blueD{-8m} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract 8m from each side.}}\\\\
12.6-4m&=9.6 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
12.6-4m\blueD{-12.6} &= 9.6\blueD{-12.6} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract 12.6 from each side.}}\\\\
-4m &=-3 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{-4m}{\blueD{-4}} &= \dfrac{-3}{\blueD{-4}} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by -4.}}\\\\
m &= \greenD{0.75} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$m = \greenD{0.75     }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]

items.question_content:
**Solve for $b$.**

$\dfrac23b + 5 = 20 - b$

$b =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: We need to manipulate the equation to get $b$ by itself.

$\begin{align} \dfrac23b + 5 &= 20 - b \\\\
\dfrac23b + 5 \blueD{+b} &= 20 - b \blueD{+ b} &&\small\gray{\text{Add b to each side.}}\\\\
\dfrac53b+5 &= 20 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac53b+5 \blueD{-5} &= 20 \blueD{- 5} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract 5 from each side.}}\\\\
\dfrac53b &= 15 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac53b\cdot \blueD{\dfrac35}&= 15 \cdot \blueD{\dfrac35}&&\small\gray{\text{Multiply each side by}\dfrac35.} \\\\
b &= \greenD{9} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$b = \greenD{9}~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]

items.question_content:
**Solve for $n$.**

$2-\dfrac12n=3n+16$

$n=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: We need to manipulate the equation to get $   n   $ by itself.

$\begin{align} 2-\dfrac12n&=3n+16 \\\\
2-\dfrac12n \blueD{-3n} &= 3n+16\blueD{-3n} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract 3n from each side.}}\\\\
-\dfrac72n+2&=16 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
-\dfrac72n+2\blueD{-2} &= 16\blueD{-2} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract 2 from each side.}}\\\\
-\dfrac72n &=14 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
-\dfrac72n\cdot\left(\blueD{-\dfrac27}\right) &= 14\cdot\left(\blueD{-\dfrac27}\right) &&\small\gray{\text{Multiply each side by }-\dfrac27.}\\\\
n&=-\dfrac{28}7\\\\
n &= \greenD{-4} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$ n= \greenD{   -4   }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]

items.question_content:
**Solve for $p$.**

$16-3p=\dfrac23p+5$

$p=$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: We need to manipulate the equation to get $   p   $ by itself.

$\begin{align} 16-3p&=\dfrac23p+5 \\\\
16-3p \blueD{-\dfrac23p} &= \dfrac23p+5\blueD{-\dfrac23p} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract }\dfrac23p \text{ from each side.}}\\\\
-\dfrac{11}{3}p+16&=5 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
-\dfrac{11}{3}p+16\blueD{-16} &= 5\blueD{-16} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract 16 from each side.}}\\\\
-\dfrac{11}{3}p &=-11 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
-\dfrac{11}{3}p\cdot\left(\blueD{-\dfrac{3}{11}}\right) &= -11\cdot\left(\blueD{-\dfrac{3}{11}}\right) &&\small\gray{\text{Multiply each side by -}\dfrac{3}{11}.}\\\\
p&=\dfrac{33}{11}\\\\\\
p &= \greenD{3} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$ p= \greenD{   3   }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]

items.question_content:
**Solve for $r$.**

$12-\dfrac15r=2r+1$

$r=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: We need to manipulate the equation to get $ r     $ by itself.

$\begin{align} 12-\dfrac15r&=2r+1 \\\\
12-\dfrac15r \blueD{-2r} &= 2r+1\blueD{-2r} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract 2r from each side.}}\\\\
-\dfrac{11}5r+12&=1 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
-\dfrac{11}5r+12\blueD{-12} &= 1\blueD{-12} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract 12 from each side.}}\\\\
-\dfrac{11}5r &=-11 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
-\dfrac{11}{5}r\cdot\left(\blueD{-\dfrac{5}{11}}\right) &= -11\cdot\left(\blueD{-\dfrac{5}{11}}\right) &&\small\gray{\text{Multiply each side by -}\dfrac{5}{11}.}\\\\
r&=\dfrac{55}{11}\\\\\\
r &= \greenD{5} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$r = \greenD{5      }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]

items.question_content:
**Solve for $s$.**

$2-2s=\dfrac34s+13$

$s=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: We need to manipulate the equation to get $   s   $ by itself.

$\begin{align} 2-2s&=\dfrac34s+13 \\\\
2-2s \blueD{-\dfrac34s} &= \dfrac34s+13\blueD{-\dfrac34s} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract }\dfrac34s \text{ from each side.}}\\\\
-\dfrac{11}4 s+2&=13 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
-\dfrac{11}4s+2\blueD{-2} &= 13\blueD{-2} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract 2 from each side.}}\\\\
-\dfrac{11}4s &=11 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
-\dfrac{11}{4}s\cdot\left(\blueD{-\dfrac{4}{11}}\right) &= 11\cdot\left(\blueD{-\dfrac{4}{11}}\right) &&\small\gray{\text{Multiply each side by -}\dfrac{4}{11}.}\\\\
s&=-\dfrac{44}{11}\\\\\
s &= \greenD{-4} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$s = \greenD{     -4 }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]

items.question_content:
**Solve for $t$.**

$16-2t=\dfrac32t+9$

$t=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: We need to manipulate the equation to get $   t   $ by itself.

$\begin{align} 16-2t&=\dfrac32t+9 \\\\
16-2t \blueD{-\dfrac32t} &= \dfrac32t+9\blueD{-\dfrac32t} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract }\dfrac32 t \text{ from each side.}}\\\\
-\dfrac72t+16&=9 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
-\dfrac72t+16\blueD{-16} &= 9\blueD{-16} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract 16 from each side.}}\\\\
-\dfrac72t &=-7 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
-\dfrac{7}{2}t\cdot\left(\blueD{-\dfrac{2}{7}}\right) &= -7\cdot\left(\blueD{-\dfrac{2}{7}}\right) &&\small\gray{\text{Multiply each side by -}\dfrac{2}{7}.}\\\\
t&=\dfrac{14}{7}\\\\\
t &= \greenD{2} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$ t= \greenD{  2    }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]

items.question_content:
**Solve for $a$.**

$-\dfrac14a-4=\dfrac74a-3$

$a=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: We need to manipulate the equation to get $   a   $ by itself.

$\begin{align} -\dfrac14a-4&=\dfrac74a-3 \\\\
-\dfrac14a-4 \blueD{-\dfrac74a} &= \dfrac74a-3\blueD{-\dfrac74a} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract} \dfrac74a \text{ from each side.}}\\\\
-2a-4&=-3 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
-2a-4\blueD{+4} &= -3\blueD{+4} &&\small\gray{\text{Add 4 to each side.}}\\\\
-2a &=1 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{-2a}{\blueD{-2}} &= \dfrac{1}{\blueD{-2}} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by -2.}}\\\\
a &= \greenD{-\dfrac12} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$ a= \greenD{-\dfrac12      }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]

items.question_content:
**Solve for $b$.**

$7b-\dfrac25=6b-\dfrac75$

$b=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: We need to manipulate the equation to get $  b    $ by itself.

$\begin{align} 7b-\dfrac25&=6b-\dfrac75 \\\\
7b-\dfrac25 \blueD{-6b} &= 6b-\dfrac75\blueD{-6b} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract 6b from each side.}}\\\\
1b-\dfrac25&=-\dfrac75 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
1b-\dfrac25\blueD{+\dfrac25} &= -\dfrac75\blueD{+\dfrac25} &&\small\gray{\text{Add }\dfrac25\text{ to each side.}}\\\\
1b &=-1 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{1b}{\blueD{1}} &= \dfrac{-1}{\blueD{1}} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by 1}}\\\\
b &= \greenD{-1} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$b = \greenD{ -1     }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]

items.question_content:
**Solve for $c$.**

$\dfrac83c-2=\dfrac23 c-12$

$c=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: We need to manipulate the equation to get $  c    $ by itself.

$\begin{align} \dfrac83c-2&=\dfrac23c-12 \\\\
\dfrac83c-2 \blueD{-\dfrac23c} &= \dfrac23c-12\blueD{-\dfrac23c} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract } \dfrac23c \text{ from each side.}}\\\\
2c-2&=-12 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
2c-2\blueD{+2} &= -12\blueD{+2} &&\small\gray{\text{Add 2 to each side.}}\\\\
2c &=-10 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{2c}{\blueD{2}} &= \dfrac{-10}{\blueD{2}} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by 2.}}\\\\
c &= \greenD{-5} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$c = \greenD{  -5    }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]

items.question_content:
**Solve for $d$.**

$6d-\dfrac{11}2=2d-\dfrac{13}2$

$d=$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: We need to manipulate the equation to get $  d    $ by itself.

$\begin{align} 6d-\dfrac{11}2&=2d-\dfrac{13}2 \\\\
6d-\dfrac{11}2 \blueD{-2d} &= 2d-\dfrac{13}2\blueD{-2d} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract 2d from each side.}}\\\\
4d-\dfrac{11}2&=-\dfrac{13}2 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
4d-\dfrac{11}2\blueD{+\dfrac{11}2} &= -\dfrac{13}2\blueD{+\dfrac{11}2} &&\small\gray{\text{Add }\dfrac{11}2\text{ to each side.}}\\\\
4d &=-1 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{4d}{\blueD{4}} &= \dfrac{-1}{\blueD{4}} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by 4.}}\\\\
d &= \greenD{-\dfrac14} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$ d= \greenD{   -\dfrac14   }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]

items.question_content:
**Solve for $y$.**

$\dfrac94y-12=\dfrac14y-4$

$y=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: We need to manipulate the equation to get $  y    $ by itself.

$\begin{align} \dfrac94y-12&=\dfrac14y-4 \\\\
\dfrac94y-12 \blueD{-\dfrac14y} &= \dfrac14y-4\blueD{-\dfrac14y} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract } \dfrac14y \text{ from each side.}}\\\\

khan_exercises.title: Linear equations with unknown coefficients
khan_exercises.description: Solve a linear equation where the coefficients are represented by letters.

items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$.**  
*Assume the equation has a solution for $x$.*

$ax+3x = bx+5$

$x=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $x$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $x$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $x$.

###Solving for $x$

$\begin{align} ax+3x &= bx+5  \\\\
ax+3x-bx &= 5 & (\text{Subtract } bx \text{ from both sides}) \\\\ 
x(a+3-b) &= 5 & (\text{Factor out }x) \\\\ 
x &= \dfrac{5}{a+3-b} & (\text{Divide both sides by } a+3-b)\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###The answer

$x = \dfrac{5}{a+3-b} $

items.question_content:
**Solve for $y$.**  
*Assume the equation has a solution for $y$.*

$py+qy= -4y+8$

$y=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $y$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $y$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $y$.

###Solving for $y$

$\begin{align} py+qy&= -4y+8 \\\\
py+qy+4y&= 8 & (\text{Add } 4y \text{ to both sides}) \\\\ 
y(p+q+4) &= 8 & (\text{Factor out }y) \\\\ 
y &= \dfrac{8}{p+q+4} & (\text{Divide both sides by } p+q+4)\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###The answer

$y = \dfrac{8}{p+q+4} $

items.question_content:
**Solve for $z$.**  
*Assume the equation has a solution for $z$.*

$-cz+6z= tz+83$

$z=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $z$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $z$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $z$.

###Solving for $z$

$\begin{align} -cz+6z&= tz+83  \\\\
-cz+6z-tz&= 83 & (\text{Subtract } tz \text{ from both sides}) \\\\ 
z(-c+6-t) &= 83 & (\text{Factor out }z) \\\\ 
z &= \dfrac{83}{-c+6-t} & (\text{Divide both sides by } -c+6-t)\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###The answer

$z = \dfrac{83}{-c+6-t} $

items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$.**  
*Assume the equation has a solution for $x$.*

$19x+rx= -37x+w$

$x=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $x$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $x$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $x$.

###Solving for $x$

$\begin{align} 19x+rx&= -37x+w  \\\\
37x+19x+rx&= w & (\text{Add } 37x \text{  to both sides}) \\\\ 
56x+rx&=w\\\\
x(56+r) &= w & (\text{Factor out }x) \\\\ 
x &= \dfrac{w}{56+r} & (\text{Divide both sides by } 56+r)\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###The answer

items.question_content:
**Solve for $z$.**  
*Assume the equation has a solution for $z$.*

$16z+29z= pz-v$

$z=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $z$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $z$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $z$.

###Solving for $z$

$\begin{align} 16z+29z&= pz-v  \\\\
45z-pz&= -v & (\text{Subtract } pz \text{ from both sides}) \\\\ 
z(45-p) &= -v & (\text{Factor out }z) \\\\ 
z &= \dfrac{-v}{45-p} & (\text{Divide both sides by } 45-p)\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###The answer

$z = \dfrac{-v}{45-p} $

items.question_content:
**Solve for $y$.**  
*Assume the equation has a solution for $y$.*

$py+7 = 6y+q$

$y=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $y$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $y$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $y$.

###Solving for $y$

$\begin{align} py+7 &= 6y+q  \\\\
py+7 -6y &= q & (\text{Subtract } 6y \text{ from both sides}) \\\\ 
py-6y&= q - 7& (\text{Subtract } 7 \text{ from both sides})\\\\
y(p-6) &= q-7 & (\text{Factor out }y) \\\\ 
y &= \dfrac{q-7}{p-6} & (\text{Divide both sides by } p-6)\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###The answer

$y = \dfrac{q-7}{p-6}$

items.question_content:
**Solve for $z$.**  
*Assume the equation has a solution for $z$.*

$-4z+1 = bz+c$

$z=$
 [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $z$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $z$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $z$.

###Solving for $z$

$\begin{align} -4z+1 &= bz+c  \\\\
-4z+1 -bz &= c & (\text{Subtract } bz \text{ from both sides}) \\\\ 
-4z-bz&= c -1& (\text{Subtract } 1 \text{ from both sides})\\\\
z(-4-b) &= c-1 & (\text{Factor out }z) \\\\ 
z &= \dfrac{c-1}{-4-b} & (\text{Divide both sides by } -4-b)\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###The answer

$z = \dfrac{c-1}{-4-b}$

items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$.**

$-px+r = -8x-2$

$x=$
 [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $x$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $x$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $x$.

$\begin{align} -px+r &= -8x-2  \\\\
-px+r +8x &= -2 & (\text{Add } 8x \text{ to both sides}) \\\\ 
-px+8x&= -2 - r& (\text{Subtract } r \text{ from both sides})\\\\

items.question_content:
**Solve for $y$.**

$12y+d = -19y+t$

$y=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $y$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $y$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $y$.

$\begin{align} 12y+d &= -19y+t  \\\\
19y+12y+d  &= t & (\text{Add } 19y \text{ to both sides}) \\\\ 
31y&= t - d& (\text{Subtract } d \text{ from both sides})\\\\

items.question_content:
**Solve for $z$.**

$az+17 = -4z-b$

$z=$
 [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $z$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $z$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $z$.

###Solving for $z$

$\begin{align} az+17 &= -4z-b  \\\\
az+17 +4z &= -b & (\text{Add } 4z \text{ to both sides}) \\\\ 
az+4z&= -b - 17& (\text{Subtract } 17 \text{ from both sides})\\\\
z(a+4)&= -b-17 & (\text{Factor out }z) \\\\

items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$.**  
*Assume the equation has a solution for $x$.*

$a\cdot(5-x) = bx-8$

$x=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $x$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $x$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $x$.

###Solving for $x$

$\begin{align} a(5-x) &= bx-8  \\\\
5a-ax&=bx-8&(\text{Distribute over } a)\\\\
5a-ax-bx &= -8 & (\text{Subtract } bx \text{ from both sides}) \\\\ 
-ax-bx&=-8-5a&(\text{Subtract } 5a \text{ from both sides})\\\\
x(-a-b) &= -8-5a& (\text{Factor out }x) \\\\ 
x &= \dfrac{-8-5a}{-a-b} & (\text{Divide both sides by } -a-b)\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###The answer

$x = \dfrac{-8-5a}{-a-b} $

items.question_content:
**Solve for $y$.**  
*Assume the equation has a solution for $y$.*

$a\cdot(n+y) = 10y+32$

$y=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $y$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $y$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $y$.

###Solving for $y$

$\begin{align} a(n+y) &= 10y+32  \\\\
an+ay&=10y+32&(\text{Distribute over } a)\\\\
an+ay-10y &= 32 & (\text{Subtract } 10y \text{ from both sides}) \\\\ 
ay-10y&=32-an&(\text{Subtract } an \text{ from both sides})\\\\
y(a-10) &= 32-an & (\text{Factor out }y) \\\\ 
y &= \dfrac{32-an}{a-10} & (\text{Divide both sides by } a-10)\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###The answer

$y = \dfrac{32-an}{a-10} $

items.question_content:
**Solve for $z$.**  
*Assume the equation has a solution for $z$.*

$w\cdot(-4+z) = mz+17$

$z=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints:
###The strategy

- Move all $z$-terms on one side of the equation, and move all constant terms to the other side of the equation.

- Using the distributive property, group like terms together on both sides of the equation.

- To isolate $z$, divide both sides by the coefficient of $z$.

###Solving for $z$

$\begin{align} w(-4+z) &= mz+17  \\\\
